annotations.contextBefore: A reviewer who finds themselves writing three paragraphs in the margin has discovered that
annotations.contextAfter: — it belongs in a separate document, addressed to the author rather than to the page.
annotations.note: What does 'not local' mean here? Either define it (spans multiple sections, depends on global structure, …) or rephrase.

annotations.contextBefore: A page covered in marginalia is a page asking to be rewritten, not a page that has been improved.
annotations.contextAfter: noted somewhere that the proliferation of glosses on a text is a sign of its declining authority
annotations.note: 'Bourdieu noted somewhere' is exactly the kind of half-citation a reviewer should flag — name the work and the page.
